study_group_representative.quote: As of March 5, 2020, a total of 117 COVID-19 cases had been identified in Singapore. This report analyzes the first 100 COVID-19 patients in Singapore

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: This report analyzes the first 100 COVID-19 patients in Singapore to determine the effectiveness of the surveillance and containment measures.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: Among the first 100 confirmed COVID-19 cases in Singapore... The mean interval from symptom onset to hospital isolation or quarantine was 5.6 days

follow_up_length_reported.quote: The effectiveness of Singapore’s surveillance and containment efforts was assessed from the outbreak’s start until February 29

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: All analyses were conducted using R statistical software (version 3.5.1; The R Foundation).

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: Effectiveness of these measures was assessed by calculating the 7-day moving average of the interval from symptom onset to isolation in hospital or quarantine

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: Differences in the percentages of cases detected through the different surveillance components were tested using the chi-squared or Fisher’s exact test.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: The 7-day moving average of the interval from symptom onset to isolation declined significantly across the study period for both imported and local cases, from 9.0 and 18.0 days to 0.9 and 3.1 days, respectively (p<0.001)
